prep.summary: Your last meeting with Mike on May 28 focused on organizational uncertainty—specifically surrounding leadership priorities and the future of the Enterprise Cloud Platform Architecture (ECPA). You discussed shifting data architectural oversight away from Chris—while also addressing the onboarding of Marissa's team to support technical architecture needs. No work notes matched the last meeting with Aaron—requiring you to gather updates on his active tracks during the session.
tasks: Review the status of Michael Halleck's ECPA assessment—determining if the platform will receive funding or prioritization.
Schedule the onboarding alignment session with Marissa and her team—easing current architecture bandwidth constraints.
Reassess data architectural oversight transition plans—ensuring continuity after the responsibilities shifted away from Chris.
Request an update regarding Aaron's recent activities—covering his current progress and outstanding deliverables due to the lack of local notes.
prep.title: Bi-weekly 1:1 w/Mike Wilkins | Mark Francis

prep.summary: You have only one recorded meeting note with Praveena in the provided files—the June 25 session—preventing a full four-meeting historical summary. In this June 25 meeting, you coordinated on staffing and the critical July 1 resource roll-off—focusing on securing Dan's approval for the pending job requisition. You also aligned on budget strategies to justify the immediate backfill and established OOO coverage for the upcoming All People Leaders meeting.
tasks: Secure leadership approval from Dan for the pending job requisition before the July 1 resource roll-off.
Hand over the polished job description template to Talent Acquisition immediately upon funding approval.
Confirm Praveena has the necessary context to cover the All People Leaders meeting during your absence.
Follow up on the executive meet-and-greets and the associated architect coordination list submitted to Rebecca.
prep.title: Mid-Year Check-In w/Praveena | Mark

prep.summary: You need to track the recent pivot on the Task Management RFP technical decision—originally owned by Charles Ballasting—which is now fully transitioned to the Property & Casualty team. P&C is actively reworking the scope and developing their own business case—effectively ending the centralized initiative approach. Additionally—with Amit going out of office for several weeks—you must execute a final workload transition during your next 1:1 and secure a backup for the SGV portfolio.
tasks: Reassign the Task Management technical decision scope to reflect the new Property & Casualty focus.
Execute a comprehensive workload download with Amit during your upcoming 1:1 to cover his extended absence.
Solicit a volunteer from the team to replace Matt Unsworth as the SGV portfolio Active Directory group backup.
Update the team wiki to align precisely with the active SGV portfolio Active Directory group membership.
prep.title: Control Test Failure / Issue

events.summary: EMG Engage - Intro to Architecture w/Mark Francis | Villalon, Lisa

prep.summary: Review your key operational shifts from the past two weeks—including the P&C team taking over the task management RFP, the implementation of the new "Go TPDT" tech procurement workflow, and the high-stakes $15 million CMT telematics POC. Address the severe P&C pricing data crisis identified in your sync with Adam and Dan—where underlying legacy defects in the Snowflake migration threaten up to a $1 billion business impact—and plan to tackle the highly fragmented "Hydra" operating model. Note that no work notes matched your last meeting with Jon—you will need to request a direct status update from Deepak on this front.
tasks: Resolve the volunteer backup role for the SGV portfolio Active Directory group—previously managed by Matt Unsworth—before Amit begins his out-of-office leave.
Monitor the mandated rebuild of the 2018 dwelling loss model—transitioning from flat to hierarchical architecture—to mitigate the $250 million annual mainframe carry cost.
Establish clear architectural responsibilities for renewals and trial guidelines—specifically updating the "Technology Engagement in Procurement" wiki—to finalize the new intake process.
Investigate the compliance and security hurdles delaying the Visual Voicemail and Two-Way Texting initiative—currently stalled under HiMarley integration—before the targeted Q4 launch.
Align with Deepak on the status of your last discussion with Jon—given that no matching work notes were found in your Google Drive folder.
prep.title: Bi-weekly - 1:1 w/Mark Francis | Deepak
